rounds.thesis: Prior rounds decoded charts as price patterns; this round decodes the bar stream as an information channel and imports resolution dynamics from outside finance. The unifying bet: the surviving edge above the fee floor lives not in mean price behavior but in second-order structure — the entropy collapse before a directional impulse, the aftershock clustering after a shock, the geometry of a single failed auction printed in one bar's wick, the magnetic snap through a psychological level, and the system's own regime state as a conditioning prior. Each idea targets amplitude-per-fire over 1% by isolating conditions that PRECEDE follow-through rather than fading exhausted moves, deliberately avoiding both indicator families and all five documented retail lineages.

ideas.name: entropy_collapse_impulse
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if median absolute price excursion in the 24h after entry is under 1.2%, or if the +5% target is hit on fewer than 22% of fires while the -3% stop hits more than 40% — meaning the entropy-break offers no directional follow-through edge over a coin flip net of fees.

ideas.name: omori_aftershock_ladder
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if, conditional on entry, forward returns show no directional persistence — specifically if the sign-correlation between the mainshock direction and the 12-bar forward return is under +0.1, or if net-of-fee expectancy per fire is below +0.3%, proving aftershocks are noise not clustered continuation.

ideas.name: failed_auction_rejection_wick
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if the +5% target is hit less than 25% of the time while stops hit more than the target, OR if net expectancy per fire is under +0.4% — indicating the wick geometry does not identify genuine trapped flow and rejections are followed by continuation of the original push as often as reversal.

ideas.name: round_number_overshoot_snap
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if reversion moves after qualifying overshoots average under 1% before reversing, or if net-of-fee expectancy is under +0.3%/fire, or if false-break continuation (price runs further past the level after our entry) triggers the -3% stop more often than the +5% target by a factor over 1.3.

ideas.name: drawdown_regime_contrarian_gate
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if gated breakouts show no better net expectancy than un-gated ones — specifically if the difference in per-fire net expectancy between gate-open and gate-closed conditions is under +0.5%, proving the null-arm regime signal carries no exploitable information; or if armed-fire expectancy is itself below +0.3%/fire net of fees.

rounds.thesis: This round attacks the r001 post-mortem head-on: every prior death was either a fade-shaped entry against an asymmetric exit, a signal traded at the wrong phase (entropy LEVEL not delta, seismic DECAY not growth), or the best-ever meta-gate wasted on a weak engine. So this round systematically inverts each dead polarity — trading conditional-entropy SURGES as continuation instead of low-entropy coils, importing epidemiology's GROWTH/ignition (R0>1) instead of seismology's decay, reading absorption-shelf BREAKS instead of wick fades, trading named expiry-pin RELEASES instead of generic coils — and executes the explicit r001 exploit prescription by mounting a strong signed-volume ignition engine behind the proven-informative system-saturation meta-gate. The unifying bet is that amplitude-per-fire, not frequency, is manufactured by entering at genuine phase transitions (information arrival, contagion ignition, liquidity exhaustion, mechanical-suppressor removal) where follow-through — not reversion — is the base case, which is the only shape the +5%/-3%/24h exit pays for.

ideas.name: conditional_entropy_regime_expansion
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.3%/trade OR if the continuation win rate after an entropy-jump bar is statistically indistinguishable from the null_baseline arm (i.e. the entropy delta carries no directional information beyond the vol filter alone — test by ablating the entropy term).

ideas.name: epidemic_r0_crossover_ignition
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.3%/trade OR if fires cluster so tightly under the 1h cooldown that effective sample < 30 in 6 months (starved like the dead lead-lag idea) OR if R0>1.5 crossings show no forward-return edge over the median-volume filter alone.

ideas.name: absorption_shelf_breakout
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.4%/trade OR if post-shelf breaks reverse back through the shelf (false breaks) more than 55% of the time, indicating the shelf is not absorption but distribution — measured directly as the fraction of fires that hit the -3% stop before +5%.

ideas.name: options_expiry_pin_release
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.4%/trade OR if post-08:00 breaks show no larger forward move than breaks at random non-expiry timestamps (i.e. the expiry timestamp carries no excess release energy — test by comparing to a placebo window at, say, 20:00 UTC).

ideas.name: rejection_streak_gated_ignition
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if armed expectancy < +0.3%/trade (the exact bar its predecessor failed) OR if the gated engine shows no improvement over the ungated R0-ignition engine (idea 2) — i.e. the gate adds no lift, proving the self-referential regime signal does not concentrate amplitude.

rounds.thesis: This round abandons the fade shapes and coil-releases that have died repeatedly, and instead systematically explores follow-through-through-discontinuity: entries at moments of book ACCEPTANCE (held gaps, session handoffs, retested level-flips) rather than exhaustion or rejection. Three ideas share a family thread — a decisive-bar continuation engine — but attack different failure lessons: one applies it raw (microstructure), one anchors it to institutional time (calendar), one gates it with the twice-proven meta regime signal that the prompt explicitly asked to pair with a better engine. The remaining two attack the fee floor from opposite ends of the design space: the multi-day-horizon idea resurrects Omori's dead shock-detection premise but escapes its 24h fee grave via the newly-honored 120h/12% exit lever where costs are a rounding error, and the cost-engineering idea attacks the denominator directly with a patient maker-side limit retest that lets price come to the broken level. Every fire rate is derived from base-rate arithmetic with positively-correlated conjuncts to avoid the conjunction-to-zero trap, and every idea names the exact gauntlet number that kills it.

ideas.name: gap_fill_exhaustion_continuation
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.3%/trade over the full window, OR if held-gap continuation win rate against the +5/-3 exit is <40% (proving the held gap fades as often as it continues, collapsing it into the dead failed_auction result).

ideas.name: asian_to_london_handoff_thrust
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.3%/trade, OR if the 07:00-09:00 UTC break win rate is statistically indistinguishable from a random-hour break of the same-length prior range (proving the London hour carries no special energy — the dead_zone/options-pin result relocated).

ideas.name: slot_scarcity_conviction_gate
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if armed expectancy < +0.3%/trade over the FULL window (not just trailing 180d — the r002 overfitting-to-recency warning). Specifically killed if gated expectancy does not beat the ungated gap engine by a margin exceeding the sample noise, proving the gate adds nothing to a strong engine.

ideas.name: post_shock_multiday_drift
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.5%/trade over the window, OR if the 120h post-shock drift shows no directional edge (shock-direction 5-day return not distinguishable from the opposite direction), confirming Omori's verdict that shock persistence is fee-sized at ALL horizons, not just 24h.

ideas.name: pullback_to_breakout_level_limit
ideas.kill_criterion: Dies if net expectancy < +0.3%/trade, OR if retest-then-continuation win rate is no better than chasing the same breakout at market net of the assumed fill improvement (proving the retest just re-enters failing breakouts and the patience/limit advantage is illusory). Also killed if fill rate <20% within the 2h window (thesis starves).

rounds.thesis: This round attacks the recurring failure that every prior engine either starves or enters at the move's midpoint, while the self-referential gate keeps winning with nothing worthy behind it. The unifying move is INVERSION and EARLIER-TIMING: where a lens died on a fade, we propose its continuation inverse (round-number breach not snap; one-sided expansion not absorption); where imports died entering on the visible impulse (Omori, R0), we time the branching-birth loading phase before displacement; where sign-entropy had no headroom, we switch to magnitude-compressibility which volatility-clustering guarantees has headroom; and critically, we honor the standing prescription by pairing the proven regime gate with a genuine momentum-persistence engine firing >=1/sym/day so the armed sample finally reaches decidability. Every fire-rate estimate refuses to multiply anti-correlated conjuncts and instead states the joint/sequential transition rate directly, and two ideas carry explicit pre-gauntlet data checks (magnitude headroom, coil-breach base rate) so a histogram can kill them before a full run.

ideas.name: trend_persistence_regime_gated_engine
ideas.kill_criterion: Armed expectancy < +0.5%/trade over the full 930d window with n>=100 armed trades. If the better engine ALSO cannot lift armed expectancy above the fee floor at n>=100, the gate's information is not convertible by any continuation engine and the gate family should be retired.

ideas.name: return_magnitude_compressibility_break
ideas.kill_criterion: Either (a) pre-check kills it: the surprise metric shows no headroom (p99 surprise < 3.0 impossible to reach, or quiet-then-surprise transition fires <0.01/sym/day = starved); or (b) full gauntlet expectancy < +0.4%/trade at n>=50. The sign of the breaking bar carrying no continuation edge (like post_shock_multiday_drift) is the decisive kill.

ideas.name: hawkes_self_excitation_intensity_entry
ideas.kill_criterion: Expectancy < +0.4%/trade at n>=100 armed fires. Specifically, if the win rate is no better than entering ON the impulse (compare to the omori/r0 ~40% figures), then early intensity timing captures nothing the late entry didn't — the loading phase is not tradable and the Hawkes import joins Omori and R0 in the shock-family graveyard for good.

ideas.name: one_sided_range_expansion_thrust
ideas.kill_criterion: Expectancy < +0.4%/trade at n>=200 (it fires plenty, so n is not the issue). If a clean one-sided thrust continues no better than a random large bar (win rate near the exit's breakeven ~38%), the geometry is decorative and the microstructure-from-OHLCV lens is refuted for continuation as it was for absorption/fades.

ideas.name: round_number_breach_continuation
ideas.kill_criterion: Expectancy < +0.4%/trade at n>=100. Decisively: if round-number breaches with volume confirmation perform no better than the dead dead_zone/asian_london coil breaks (which resolved at random vs the fee floor), then round numbers carry no continuation energy either and the behavioral-calendar price-level thesis is refuted alongside its clock-time cousins.

rounds.thesis: This round abandons every exhausted entry geometry (candle-shape microstructure, calendar coils, point-process imports, sign-entropy) and every conjunctive gate that starved four consecutive samples. The unifying move is to stop asking candles for direction and instead exploit two structural facts the record has NOT yet tested: (1) the self-referential gate's power came from a SINGLE observable — random entries failing — so this round defines every meta-condition as a single scalar threshold, never a conjunction, deliberately over-firing so armed samples clear n>=100; and (2) the only measured-open OHLCV band is 24h+ maker-friendly holds and 2-7 day persistence, so three of five ideas relocate entirely to the multi-day regime where the fee floor is a rounding error and where the question shifts from 'which direction does this bar resolve' to 'is this symbol in a persistent trending state that will still be trending days from now.' No idea here enters ON an impulse; they enter on the PERSISTENCE of a state, which resolves slower than the impulse and outruns the cost floor by amortization rather than amplitude-per-bar.

ideas.name: placebo_losing_streak_single_gate_trend
ideas.kill_criterion: Killed if armed win rate <= 40% over n>=100 (matching the asymmetric-exit breakeven), OR if net expectancy < +0.2%/trade. If the single-condition gate ALSO fails to lift win rate above the conjunctive gates' 25-43% band, the entire self-referential family is dead not just its conjunctive form.

ideas.name: trailing_return_rank_persistence_hold
ideas.kill_criterion: Killed if win rate (tp-before-sl) <= 33% over n>=100 (the +15/-6 breakeven is ~29%, so 33% leaves no margin after fees), OR if net expectancy < +0.5%/trade. If cross-sectional leadership shows NO continuation edge at 7-day horizon, the published-persistence prior is refuted for this basket.

ideas.name: weekly_pullback_limit_into_uptrend
ideas.kill_criterion: Killed if maker limit-fill win rate (tp-before-sl) <= 33% over n>=100, OR net expectancy < +0.5%/trade. If pullbacks-in-weekly-uptrends select failures the way intraday retests did (r003), win rate collapses below 33% and the horizon-fix is refuted.

ideas.name: placebo_streak_gated_weekly_trend_engine
ideas.kill_criterion: Killed if armed win rate <= ungated engine win rate (gate adds nothing) over n>=100, OR if armed net expectancy < +0.5%/trade. Decisively: if this — the single-gate + multi-day-engine combination, both prior failure modes removed — still cannot beat its ungated engine at n>=100, the entire gate family is retired permanently.

ideas.name: multiday_magnitude_persistence_directional_hold
ideas.kill_criterion: Killed if win rate (tp-before-sl) <= 33% over n>=100, OR net expectancy < +0.5%/trade, OR if qualifying-vs-non-qualifying symbols show no win-rate difference (magnitude persistence adds no regime-quality information). The pre-implementation premise check must confirm lag-1 |return| autocorrelation actually reaches 0.25+ on ~40%+ of symbol-days before spending the full gauntlet.
